codeReview.summary: The module persists prior-day as_of_timestamp values, ABS-adjusted framing, and defensive-efficiency histories, but it does not satisfy the per-game/rolling DRS-OAA contract with the real inputs: the production fielding and framing fetchers only provide season leaderboards, and the fallback path turns those full-season totals into one synthetic prior-day observation.

issues.description: When the real fetchers are used, `_normalize_fielding_frame()` and `_normalize_framing_frame()` receive season-level leaderboards from `fetch_fielding_stats()` and `fetch_catcher_framing()` (`src/clients/statcast_client.py:176-200` and `236-261`), not per-game rows with dates. `_build_metric_histories()` therefore falls into the no-date branch at lines 489-494, sums the full-season DRS/OAA/framing totals, divides by prior games played, and stamps a single synthetic row on `target_day - 1`. That leaks future season performance into historical dates and makes the 30/60 windows collapse to the same value as the season snapshot, so the implementation does not actually compute team DRS/OAA/framing per game date or real 30/60 rolling windows.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `feature-worker` skill still lacks the Windows-safe environment and validation guidance that workers on this repo need, even though the shared environment docs and `data-worker` already document it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\skills\feature-worker\SKILL.md:41,50` only lists generic pytest/py_compile commands, while `C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\environment.md:57-61` and `C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\skills\data-worker\SKILL.md:19-34` document the Windows `.factory/init.sh` / `pip` PATH failure and the direct `.venv\Scripts\python.exe` fallback. The handoff `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-19T17-45-54-243Z__t11-defense-features__0e7f5024-ab7f-4306-a839-b833046b58ba.json` records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure = false` for this exact reason.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library does not document that the current fielding/framing clients expose season snapshots rather than dated game-level defense history, which is crucial for anyone building anti-leakage rolling defense features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The library only mentions defense metrics at `C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\architecture.md:68,78`, while the actual clients fetch season leaderboards in `src/clients/statcast_client.py:176-200` and `236-261`. This feature then had to invent a snapshot fallback in `src/features/defense.py:489-494`, which is exactly where the rolling-window/leakage problem originates.

summary: Review failed. The feature records ABS-adjusted framing and prior-day `as_of_timestamp`, but with the real season-level fielding/framing sources it cannot produce true per-game DRS/OAA histories: it backfills a single pseudo row from full-season totals, which leaks future data and collapses the 30/60 windows. I also noted a feature-worker skill gap for Windows setup guidance and a missing shared note about the defense data sources' snapshot granularity.
